items.title: Can Selangor feed itself? Food Valley harvests 10 tonnes as state builds three-month food buffer
items.summary: KUALA SELANGOR, July 14 — Selangor Food Valley (SFV) has produced more than 10 tonnes of various fruits and agricultural produce so far this year as part of efforts to strengthen the state’s food security. Selangor Agricultural Development Corporation (PKPS) chief executive officer Datuk Dr Mohamad Khairil Mohamad Razi said the output comprised various fruits, including durian and rambutan, as well as corn, with total production expected to continue increasing until the end of the year. Selangor Agricultural Development Corporation group chief executive officer Datuk Dr Mohamad Khairil Mohamad Razi speaks at a media briefing at Selangor Fruit Valley in Bestari Jaya on July 14, 2026.

items.title: Ab Rauf says PH only mirrored federal unity in Melaka
items.summary: AYER KEROH: Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh has described Pakatan Harapan's inclusion in the state government as mere "decoration" to mirror the federal administration in Putrajaya, rather than an official partnership. He said Barisan Nasional never promised to form a coalition state government with any party after its landslide victory in the 2021 Melaka state election. "In reality, we did not form a government with any other party to support the administration after the state election.

items.title: Families claim bodies as Bangkok bar fire death toll rises to 30
items.summary: BANGKOK: Grieving relatives of victims of a deadly Bangkok bar fire collected their loved ones' bodies today as the death toll rose to 30, while others continued searching for missing family members. By Tuesday morning, Bangkok's Chatuchak district office confirmed the death toll had risen to 30, with 75 people injured, including 24 in critical condition. Relatives of the victims gathered at Police General Hospital on Tuesday to identify and collect the bodies of their loved ones.

items.title: In Muhyiddin’s trial, businessman says brother donated part of company’s RM13.7m to Bersatu without his knowledge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Businessman Shahrin Shamsuddin today told the High Court that he only discovered his elder brother, Shahradzi, had used part of their family-operated company’s RM13.772 million to donate to Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) after the donations had already been made. Shahrin said this while testifying as the 15th prosecution witness in Bersatu president and former prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s power abuse and money laundering trial. After receiving security deposits totalling RM14.5 million from Sutracom’s subcontractors in 10 transactions between September 2021 and November 2022, Shahrin transferred RM13.772 million from Sutracom to his brother’s company, Mamfor Sdn Bhd, through nine transactions during the same period.

items.title: Asean envoy meets Myanmar opposition groups in Thailand
items.summary: BANGKOK: Asean's special envoy on Myanmar held talks with ethnic minority rebel groups and a government-backed negotiation committee in an effort to tackle a civil war, with both sides expressing openness to dialogue, the Philippines said on Tuesday. The talks took place in Thailand on Monday and followed a separate meeting a day earlier between foreign ministers of the 11-member Asean and their Myanmar counterpart, their first face-to-face talks since a 2021 military coup in Myanmar that triggered nationwide conflict. But some analysts have said that re-engaging with Myanmar's new nominally civilian government, led by former junta chief turned president, Min Aung Hlaing, could weaken Asean's leverage.

items.title: Helmetless riders during Johor campaign issued RTD investigation notices
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor Road Transport Department (RTD) has issued notices to riders caught riding motorcycles without helmets during the recent Johor election campaign. Johor RTD director Zulkarnain Yasin said no one would be spared from enforcement action regardless of political affiliation. Zulkarnain said the action followed a viral video showing a group of election campaign workers riding motorcycles without wearing helmets during the campaign period.

items.title: ‘Fat Leonard’ seeks Trump pardon, citing cancer and financial hardship
items.summary: WASHINGTON, July 14 — Former defence contractor Leonard Glenn Francis is reportedly seeking clemency from US President Donald Trump as he serves a 15-year sentence for bribery and fraud in the US Navy’s largest corruption scandal. Francis, a Malaysian citizen, reportedly said he had paid only US$5 million (about RM20 million) of the US$35 million he agreed to forfeit to the US government under his plea deal. However, he reportedly claimed that mounting legal costs and the expense of lobbying for clemency had left him unable to pay the remaining US$30 million.

items.title: 14 men claim trial over alleged cross-dressing, indecent dressing offences in Temerloh
items.summary: TEMERLOH: Fourteen men pleaded not guilty at the Syariah Lower Court here today to charges of behaving like women at a hall in Mentakab last year. Three of the accused also faced additional charges of dressing indecently in public. The men were charged under Section 33 of the Pahang Syariah Criminal Offences Enactment 2013 for dressing or acting like women.

items.title: SC to recover RM5.83mil after former WCT deputy MD, director appeal dismissed
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Securities Commission (SC) will proceed to recover the judgment sum of RM5.83 million ordered by the High Court to former WCT Bhd deputy managing director Goh Chin Liong and Ara Holdings Sdn Bhd after they lost their appeal to overturn a RM5.83 million civil judgment for insider trading. Earlier today, a three-member Court of Appeal bench comprising judges Datuk Mohamed Zaini Mazlan, Datuk Leonard David Shim and Datuk Aliza Sulaiman unanimously dismissed the duo's appeals, affirming the High Court's ruling in favour of the SC. "Previously on May 26, 2026, the SC had succeeded in its appeal to the High Court to reinstate garnishee orders against Goh and Leong, allowing the SC to enforce recovery of the RM5.83 million judgment sum arising from the insider trading breaches by both defendants.

items.title: Too popular for its own good? GOF seizes 7,200 suspected smuggled Obuse beauty products at Rantau Panjang jetty
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 14 — The General Operations Force (GOF) seized 7,200 cosmetics products worth an estimated RM144,000, suspected to have been smuggled from Thailand, in a raid at the Bilah Dollah illegal jetty in Rantau Panjang yesterday. GOF Southeast Brigade commander SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain said that during the operation at about 8pm, personnel conducting motorcycle patrols spotted a man unloading several brown boxes on the riverbank. Upon noticing the presence of GOF personnel, the suspect fled by boat towards Thailand and could not be traced.

items.title: China not seeking 'sphere of influence' with Pacific island nations, FM says
items.summary: BEIJING: China "has no geopolitical intentions and does not seek a so-called 'sphere of influence'" in its dealings with Pacific island nations, Foreign Minister Wang Yi told his counterpart from the Solomon Islands in Beijing on Tuesday. China's cooperation with the Solomon Islands "comes with no political strings attached" and is not forcibly imposed, Wang told Rick Houenipwela, an official summary released by China's foreign ministry showed. China is willing to enhance cooperation with the Solomon Islands including in green energy, healthcare and climate change, Wang said on Tuesday.

items.title: Social Work Profession Bill tabled for second reading to regulate sector
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's social work sector is to be brought under a formal regulatory framework for the first time, with the tabling of the Social Work Profession Bill for its second reading in the Dewan Rakyat. Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the legislation was necessary because Malaysia currently had no clear definition of social work services in any existing Act. She said the Bill would establish the Malaysian Social Work Profession Council as a regulatory body to issue certificates of practice to social workers, maintain a publicly accessible register of qualified practitioners, develop professional competency standards, regulate ethics and discipline through a complaints and investigation mechanism, and promote the profession nationwide.

items.title: ICC prosecutor Khan loses appeal to lift suspension by British lawyers' regulator
items.summary: THE HAGUE: International Criminal Court chief prosecutor Karim Khan, who has been accused of sexual misconduct, has lost an appeal to have his interim suspension lifted by the Bar Standards Board, which regulates British court lawyers, the body said on Tuesday. Khan had already been suspended from his position on June 8 by the ICC's governing body, prolonging a crisis at the war crimes court, which is also under U.S. sanctions over investigations into the United States and Israel. The ICC, the world's first permanent war crimes court, opened in 2002 to prosecute individuals for war crimes, crimes against humanity and genocide committed by nationals of member states or on the territory of its members.

items.title: MRL committed to implement ECRL project according to schedule
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Rail Link Sdn Bhd (MRL) is committed to completing the East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) project according to the set schedule by prioritising safety, quality and public interest. MRL in a statement today took note of media reports regarding public concerns following reports from residents of Taman Mutiara Gombak 2 regarding the safety of the construction of the Eastern Rail Link (ECRL) near their residential areas. "MRL views every complaint and concern raised by the public seriously.

items.title: China expels ex-Xinjiang official from Communist Party over graft
items.summary: BEIJING: China expelled a former top Xinjiang official from the ruling Communist Party and removed him from all his posts over corruption allegations, including nepotism, bribery, and paying for sex, state media said Tuesday. Ma Xingrui, 66, was a member of the Communist Party's Politburo, a top decision-making body headed by President Xi Jinping that effectively runs the country. The Politburo approved a report by the party's anti-corruption watchdog that set out a long list of allegations against Ma, who has been under investigation since April, state news agency Xinhua said.

items.title: Wrong place, wrong time: Turkiye coup spells life for young cadet
items.summary: ON July 15, 2016, officer cadet Alper Kalin was about to leave his base in Ankara for a friend's wedding when he was told to stay behind for an anti-terrorism exercise. A few hours later, the efforts of a rogue military faction to overthrow the government of President Recep Tayyip Erdogan would fail, and Kalin would find himself facing life behind bars. For Kalin, a trainee air force pilot who had been selected for F-16 training at Akinci base near Ankara, the past decade has been spent inside a cell measuring 12 square metres in a prison 1,000 kilometres (600 miles) away.

items.title: ‘Are you registered?’ Inquest officer in Zara Qairina case questions expert witness’ child psychology specialist status
items.summary: Dr. Noor Aishah Rosli, 51, is registered only with the Malaysian Board of Counsellors and relied on her academic and clinical qualifications in child clinical psychology to prepare Zara Qairina’s psychological autopsy report. Inquest Conducting Officer Nahra Dollah said there was no evidence to date proving that the witness is registered as a child clinical psychology specialist, but only as a registered counsellor. Nahra: “To date, we have no evidence that you are a registered child clinical psychology specialist.

items.title: MACC seeks two Bangladeshi men for ongoing corruption trial
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 14 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is tracking down two Bangladeshi men required to attend an ongoing trial at the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court involving charges under Section 16(a)(B) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission Act 2009 (Act 694). 6, Level 16, Block A, Menara City One, Jalan Munshi Abdullah, 50100 Kuala Lumpur. The Commission is also looking for Md Sumon Hossain, whose last known address was Residensi Pandanmas, Block B-20-05, Lorong Delapan, Kampung Pandan, 55100 Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Queen, Singapore First Lady tour Bangi autism centre
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, today accompanied Singapore First Lady Jane Ittogi Shanmugaratnam on a visit to the Bangi Autism Service Centre (PPA). The visit was held in conjunction with Singapore President Tharman Shanmugaratnam's state visit to Malaysia, which began yesterday, according to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's Facebook page. [[nid:1488637]] Also present were the wife of the Prime Minister, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail; Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri; Deputy Minister Lim Hui Ying; ministry secretary-general Datuk Dr Maziah Che Yusoff; and Social Welfare Department director-general Datuk Che Murad Sayang Ramjan.

items.title: New US Ebola patient arrives in Germany for treatment
items.summary: BERLIN: An American Ebola patient has arrived in Germany for treatment, the health ministry in Berlin said on Monday, weeks after another US citizen infected with Ebola was treated in Berlin. The patient landed in Frankfurt overnight and was transferred to the city's university hospital, the ministry said. "The risk of someone infected with Ebola entering Germany is very low," the ministry said.

items.title: Two years on the run ends as police nab robbery suspect in Kajang
items.summary: Cheras district police chief ACP Mohd Rosli Daud said the suspect, who is in his late 20s, was arrested during a raid in Sungai Jelok, Kajang, Selangor, at about noon last Friday. “Acting on information and continuous intelligence gathering, a team from the Criminal Investigation Department (D9) of the Cheras district police headquarters arrested the suspect by the roadside in Sungai Jelok,” he said in a statement today. He said the suspect was wanted for investigations into a gang robbery at a condominium unit in Jalan Dua, Chan Sow Lin, in March 2024.

items.title: AG-PP separation Bill postponed to next sitting pending presentation to Conference of Rulers, says Azalina
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said sought the postponement under Standing Order 62 of the Standing Orders of the Dewan Rakyat after tabling the Special Select Committee report (DR11/2026) today. Earlier, when tabling the report, Azalina, who is also chairperson of the Special Select Committee, said the government had agreed to introduce a further amendment to Clause 18 of Article 145A to further strengthen Parliament’s role in the appointment process of the Public Prosecutor. She said under the additional amendment agreed to by the Cabinet, the name of the proposed Public Prosecutor candidate must first be submitted to the Dewan Rakyat Speaker to be presented and scrutinised by a Dewan Rakyat Select Committee, before the house makes a recommendation to the Judicial and Legal Service Commission (SPKP).

items.title: 153 foreigners detained in Johor crackdown on illegal businesses
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A total of 153 foreigners were detained after the Johor Immigration Department launched a statewide crackdown on illegal business activities and immigration offences. Johor Immigration director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said the foreigners had misused Malaysia's immigration facilities to profit from unauthorised business activities. All detainees are being investigated under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and Immigration Regulations 1963 and have since been placed at the Setia Tropika Immigration Depot.

items.title: 57 gored or bruised during Spain's San Fermin bull runs
items.summary: PAMPLONA: Ten men suffered injuries Tuesday during the eighth and final bull run of Spain's famed San Fermin festival, bringing to 57 the total number of daredevils injured during this year's fiesta. The bulls completed the 848.6-metre course from a holding pen to the city bull ring in two minutes and 25 seconds. The bulls face almost certain death in afternoon bullfights featuring Spain's top matadors.

items.title: A simple lesson from Syed Saddiq case: If something belongs to you, keep it to yourself — Hafiz Hassan
items.summary: One of the charges under the PC was for an offence under Section 403 for dishonestly misappropriating the sum of RM120,000 by transferring the sum from the bank account of Armada Bumi Bersatu Enterprise (ABBE) for the accused’s own use. In the High Court, the learned trial judge had made a finding of fact that the RM120,000 was for the use of the accused to finance his election expenses. However, having found that the impugned sum was in fact for the use of the accused to finance his election expenses, the learned trial judge found that there was prima facie evidence that the accused had directed the assistant treasurer of Armada (PW13) to transfer the sum to the accused’s personal account.

items.title: Man, 65, charged in Perlis with trafficking 39.9 litres of fentanyl
items.summary: KANGAR, July 14 — An elderly man was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with trafficking 39.9 litres of fentanyl earlier this month. Him Mat, 65, nodded after the charge was read out before Magistrate Ana Rozana Mohd Nor. According to the charge sheet, he was accused of trafficking the drug with another individual who remains at large at a house in Kampung Guar Musang, Bukit Keteri, Padang Besar, at 5.50pm on July 1.

items.title: Ex-WCT deputy MD, company director lose appeal in RM5.83mil insider trading case
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Former WCT Bhd deputy managing director Goh Chin Liong and Ara Holdings Sdn Bhd director Leong Ah Chai lost their appeal to overturn a RM5.83 million civil judgment for insider trading. A three-member Court of Appeal bench comprising Justices Datuk Mohamed Zaini Mazlan, Datuk Leonard David Shim and Datuk Aliza Sulaiman unanimously dismissed the duo's appeals after finding no appealable error warranting appellate intervention, thereby affirming the High Court's ruling in favour of the Securities Commission Malaysia (SC). Following the disclosure, Leong disposed of 1.64 million WCT shares through Ara Holdings' trading account between Jan 2 and Jan 5, 2009.

items.title: Melaka to add up to seven nominated assemblymen after constitutional amendment
items.summary: MELAKA, July 14 — The Melaka State Legislative Assembly today passed a Bill to amend the State Constitution to pave the way for the appointment of up to seven nominated state assemblymen. The Melaka State Constitution (Amendment) Bill 2026 was passed with the support of 23 assemblymen, while five voted against it. Ab Rauf said the amendment aims to strengthen representation in the State Assembly by allowing the appointment of individuals with expertise and experience in fields such as law, economics, education, investment, technology and state development to contribute directly to policymaking and legislative debates.

items.title: Bahrain says intercepted multiple Iranian attacks
items.summary: MANAMA: Bahrain on Tuesday said it intercepted several Iranian attacks on its territory, as fighting between Washington and Teheran escalated over the vital Strait of Hormuz. "Air defence systems... intercepted and destroyed several treacherous aerial attacks launched by Iran this Tuesday morning," the Bahraini military said in a statement, also accusing Teheran of targeting civilians. Iran said it had targeted the Gulf nation in retaliation for US attacks on Iranian territory, with the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps earlier saying they had carried out missile and drone strikes on Bahrain.

items.title: Malaysia SMEs turn to digital platforms to tap China's travel recovery
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and tourism players are being encouraged to tap into China's recovering outbound travel market through digital platforms, as changing consumer behaviour creates new opportunities for Malaysian brands. Xiaohongshu Business, in collaboration with the Malaysia-China Chamber of Commerce (MCCC), recently hosted a series of Xiaohongshu Malaysia Chinese Business Growth Summit sessions in Kuala Lumpur, Negeri Sembilan and Melaka, attracting more than 300 entrepreneurs, tourism operators and SME representatives. In Negeri Sembilan, the discussion focused on opportunities within the tourism sector, with participants exploring ways Malaysian tourism operators could better engage Chinese travellers through digital platforms.

items.title: Elderly man charged with trafficking 39.9 litres of fentanyl
items.summary: KANGAR: An elderly man was charged in the Magistrate's Court here today with trafficking 39.9 litres of fentanyl earlier this month. Him Mat, 65, nodded after the charge was read out before Magistrate Ana Rozana Mohd Nor. The accused was denied bail, and the court fixed Oct 8 for mention of the case pending submission of the chemist's report.

items.title: Man shocked to discover accident victim was his own father
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: A man was shocked to discover that the victim killed in an accident at the Bandar Seri Cendana traffic light intersection here today was his father. Wan Muhammad Shah Danial Wan Mohd Shamsury, 25, was travelling along the road at about 8.40am when he witnessed the accident. Wan Muhammad Shah Danial said he never expected his father to be involved in the accident and die at the scene.

items.title: Proton X70 driver, 70, pleads not guilty to causing motorcyclist’s death in PJ’s Section 17
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — A senior citizen pleaded not guilty in the Petaling Jaya Magistrate’s Court today to a charge of dangerous driving that caused the death of a motorcyclist last May. Fong Heng Leong, 72, was charged with driving a Proton X70 dangerously, resulting in the death of Azim Haikal Noor Aziddin, 22, at Jalan 17/21 Petaling Jaya, Selangor at 1.42pm on May 5th. Last May, the media reported that a motorcyclist died after colliding with a sports utility vehicle (SUV) driven by an elderly man in his 70s who had lost control of the vehicle in Petaling Jaya.

items.title: How flammable decor, blocked exits turned Bangkok pub into death trap
items.summary: BANGKOK: At first, only thick smoke billowed out of the front entrance of the bar in northern Bangkok. At least 30 people have died as a result of the fire and 75 are injured, 24 of them critically. The fire erupted at 11.57pm (1657 GMT) on Sunday, probably sparked by an electrical short circuit in a ceiling air conditioner, authorities said.

items.title: Woman dies, husband injured after motorcycle hits wild boar in Bera
items.summary: BERA: A 48-year-old pillion rider died and her husband was injured after their motorcycle collided with a wild boar at Km35 of Jalan Temerloh–Bahau yesterday. Nor Harizan Muhamad Habri, who was travelling with her 50-year-old husband on a Yamaha motorcycle, succumbed to severe head injuries while receiving treatment at Sultan Haji Ahmad Shah Hospital (Hoshas) in Temerloh about 4.25am today. Harizan's husband remains hospitalised at Bera Hospital with rib injuries.

items.title: Melaka clears path to appoint seven unelected assemblymen
items.summary: AYER KEROH: The Melaka State Legislative Assembly today passed an amendment to the State Constitution to allow the appointment of up to seven assemblymen. The motion, tabled by Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh, was supported by 23 assemblymen, including three from the opposition. Under the amended Article 11 of the Melaka State Constitution, the existing 28 elected assemblymen will remain unchanged, in line with the Federal Constitution.

items.title: Johor MIC credits groundwork for election clean sweep
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor MIC's clean sweep of all four contested seats in the July 11 state election was the result of consistent groundwork and engagement with the community over the past four years, said party chief K. Raven Kumar. The newly elected Kemelah assemblyman said the party began strengthening its presence on the ground after the 2022 state election, allowing it to secure victories despite fielding three new faces in the July 11 polls. MIC won all four seats it contested — Kemelah, Kahang, Perling and Bukit Batu — with Raven saying the party's presence was felt across the state, particularly in Johor Baru, Kluang, Segamat and Kulai, where the Indian community population is significant.

items.title: Kia Malaysia expands dealer network with Cycle & Carriage flagship centre
items.summary: CYCLE & Carriage (C&C) has opened its first Kia flagship 3S centre in Malaysia, marking its entry into the Korean marque's retail network after being appointed an authorised dealer partner by Kia Sales Malaysia. C&C Malaysia chief executive officer Adrian Short said the new outlet reflects the company's strategy of strengthening aftersales support alongside vehicle sales. She said C&C's automotive experience and service capabilities would support Kia's plans to expand its presence in Malaysia.

items.title: Pekan Sehari Temerloh closure claim false, says council
items.summary: TEMERLOH: The Temerloh Municipal Council has denied claims that it instructed the closure of the popular Pekan Sehari Temerloh for three weeks. "The Temerloh Municipal Council wishes to clarify that a viral social media post claiming the 'pekan sehari' will be closed to visitors for three weeks is untrue. The decades-old Pekan Sehari Temerloh, located in Lurah Semantan, stretches 2.2km and is now the country's second longest morning market after Sungai Bayor morning market in Selama, Perak, was recognised last year as the longest at 2.3km.

items.title: KL police nab suspect linked to Chan Sow Lin gang robbery
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested a man believed to be involved in a gang robbery at a condominium unit along Jalan Dua, Chan Sow Lin, in March 2024. Cheras district police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Rosdi Daud said the suspect, who is in his late 20s, was arrested by a team from the Cheras district police headquarters' Criminal Investigation Department (D9) Special Investigation Division at about noon on July 10. Mohd Rosdi said the suspect had been remanded for seven days from July 11 until July 17 to facilitate investigations.

items.title: Zahid to launch BN election machinery, candidate lineup for Negri polls tomorrow night
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional will launch its election machinery and announce its candidates for the Negri Sembilan state election tomorrow. Yesterday, Zahid, who is also Umno president, said BN would adopt a formula tailored to the political landscape of Negri Sembilan, taking into account the state's unique demographics and voter patterns. The Election Commission has set Aug 1 as polling day for the 16th Negri Sembilan election, with all 36 state seats to be contested.

items.title: MRL declines comment on ECRL safety claims as case goes to court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Rail Link Sdn Bhd (MRL) will not comment further on safety concerns raised by residents of Taman Mutiara Gombak 2 over the construction of the East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) near their homes, as the matter is now before the courts. MRL said it had taken note of media reports on the residents' concerns and stressed that safety remained its priority in implementing the ECRL project. "Safety remains a priority in the implementation of the ECRL project," it said.

items.title: Home Ministry probes alleged immigration misuse involving Network School in Forest City
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 14 — The Home Ministry has launched a comprehensive investigation into the operations of Network School in Forest City following concerns over alleged immigration irregularities. The ministry said in a statement today that the investigation followed claims that individuals had used passports from a second country to enter Malaysia. “The Home Ministry takes seriously any matter involving national security, compliance with immigration laws and the misuse of Malaysia’s immigration facilities,” it said.

items.title: Between RM80 and RM250 for fake work permits: Kedah police uncover alleged Kulim-based document syndicate
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, July 14 — Kedah police have cracked down on a syndicate believed to be issuing fake work permits and United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cards, allegedly masterminded by foreigners. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said several raids around Kulim were carried out by the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) last month, resulting in the arrest of 19 Bangladeshi men, three Rohingya men, one Rohingya woman and a local woman aged between 20 and 52. “In the operation, police also seized 40 passports, 10 mobile phones, four laptops and one fake work permit.

items.title: TVET 2.0 to get industry boost with direct recognition for graduates
items.summary: Deputy Rural and Regional Development Minister Datuk Rubiah Wang said the strategic approach is crucial to ensuring TVET learning competency standards remain aligned with actual labour market needs, while boosting graduate employability and addressing skills mismatches. “The integrated direction of TVET under the Ministry of Rural and Regional Development (KKDW) is clear, namely to produce TVET graduates who are not only future-ready, equipped with advanced technological skills and recognised by industry, but also competent as techno-entrepreneurs and job creators capable of driving the rural economy towards national prosperity,” she said during the question-and-answer session. Rubiah said that under the TVET 2.0 framework, the ministry will also focus on human capital development more proactively, including strengthening the TVET Tahfiz programme.

items.title: Melaka DAP quits state government over nominated assemblymen Bill
items.summary: MELAKA, July 14 — Melaka DAP today announced its withdrawal from the state government in protest against the passage of a Bill allowing the appointment of nominated state assemblymen. Melaka DAP chairman Khoo Poay Tiong said the party was standing by its principles after all state Pakatan Harapan (PH) assemblymen voted against the Bill, including Kesidang assemblyman Allex Seah Shoo Chin, who serves as a state executive councillor (exco). “In keeping with the principles of parliamentary democracy, we will withdraw from the Melaka state government,” Khoo told reporters at the State Legislative Assembly in remarks broadcast live on his Facebook page.

items.title: Young Malaysians still want marriage and kids, but rising costs are delaying those plans in 2026
items.summary: National Population and Family Development Board (LPPKN) chairman Datuk Seri Rohani Abdul Karim said an LPPKN Online Public Perception Survey conducted in conjunction with World Population Day 2025, involving 3,625 Malaysians aged 18 and above, found that 90.9 per cent of single respondents intended to get married, while 91.1 per cent hoped to have children in the future. “Among married respondents, 33.8 per cent intended to have more children, with the average desired family size being 2.8 children. “However, 17.6 per cent of respondents reported that they were unable to achieve their desired number of children due to insufficient household income, rising living costs, demanding work schedules, fertility issues and health factors,” she said when officiating the national-level World Population Day 2026 at the Asian International Arbitration Centre (AIAC) today.

items.title: Penang’s eroding shoreline at Batu Ferringhi gets RM500,000 temporary protection
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 14 — A RM500,000 coastal erosion mitigation project is underway at Batu Ferringhi as a temporary measure to protect the popular beach while awaiting a permanent solution. He said the temporary measure will protect the beach until the implementation of a long-term coastal protection project approved under the 12th Malaysia Plan (12MP). He said the project would reduce erosion, stabilise the shoreline and protect public infrastructure, assets and tourism areas in Batu Ferringhi on a sustainable basis.

items.title: Rosmah sues comedian Harith Iskander over alleged defamatory jokes, body-shaming remarks in stand-up show
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor has filed a lawsuit against comedian and actor Harith Iskander, alleging she was defamed and subjected to body shaming during a stand-up comedy performance at a hotel in Melaka last January. According to Rosmah’s statement of claim, Harith performed a stand-up show titled Harith Iskander: The Outspoken Comedy Tour on January 17, during which he allegedly displayed Rosmah’s image and made defamatory remarks before a large audience for comedic effect. Rosmah claimed Harith first showed an image of a mythical supernatural creature commonly associated with Malaysian folklore before displaying her photograph.

items.title: Works Ministry explains why Sabah Pan Borneo Highway cost went from RM12.86b to RM24.89b
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — A total of RM1.2 billion has been paid by the federal government for land compensation for the Sabah Pan Borneo Highway (PBH) Phase 1 Project so far, said Deputy Works Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan. He said for the Sarawak PBH, the total land compensation payment made was RM737 million. He said the cost of Phase 1A involved 16 work packages with a total cost of RM10.9 billion, while Phase 1B involved 19 work packages with a total cost of RM13.989 billion.

items.title: Malaysia sends condolences as Zambry honours late Qatar ruler Sheikh Hamad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir arrived in Doha, Qatar early Tuesday morning to represent the Malaysian government in paying final respects to the late Father Amir of Qatar, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani. In a Facebook post, Zambry said he would be visiting the Amir of Qatar, Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, at the Lusail Palace today. Sheikh Hamad ruled Qatar from 1995 until 2013, when he handed over power to his son, Sheikh Tamim.

items.title: Housing for all? Rehda pushes for smarter planning with better data
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, July 14 — The Real Estate Housing Developers’ Association (Rehda) has called for a more dynamic, data-driven framework to help build a more balanced and inclusive housing market. Rehda Institute chairman Datuk Jeffrey Ng Tiong Lip said the government should regularly review housing quotas and policy requirements based on real market data. “Malaysia has abundant data across agencies such as the Housing Integrated Management System (HIMS), Transforming and Empowering Data Usage in Housing (Teduh), National Property Information Centre (Napic), Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) and the Central Database Hub (Padu), but much (of the data) remains fragmented.

items.title: Malaysia to work with new Thai peace envoy to advance dialogue efforts in southern Thailand
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Malaysia will work closely with Thanut Suvarnananda, the newly appointed chief of the Peace Dialogue Panel of the Royal Thai Government, to advance ongoing efforts to bring peace and stability to southern Thailand. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said Malaysia welcomed Thanut’s appointment earlier this year, expressing confidence that it would help advance the peace process through close cooperation between the two countries. Malaysia’s facilitator for the Southern Thailand Peace Dialogue is Datuk Rabin Basir.

items.title: ‘Give us the time under the mandate,’ Anwar says as pressure mounts for early GE16 after Johor polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today brushed aside calls for an early general election, urging rivals to allow his administration to complete its full term to safeguard economic growth and political stability. He said voters wanted the government to focus on running the country rather than being drawn into continuous political campaigning. The current Parliament’s term can run until February 2028 unless dissolved earlier, but speculation over an early general election has grown following political strains within the unity government.

items.title: New national housing policy to set affordable home prices using local income data
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — The government plans to use local income and housing demand data to set affordable home prices under the National Housing Policy 2026-2035. Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu said the ministry was finalising the policy as part of efforts to address rising prices and mismatches between housing supply and demand. Aiman Athirah said the ministry had mapped affordable housing prices using state- and district-level median household income data from the Household Income and Basic Amenities Survey 2024.

items.title: Housing Ministry says 1,615 troubled housing projects worth RM150.86b rehabilitated since 2022
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — The government rehabilitated 1,615 delayed, sick and abandoned housing projects involving 190,422 units with a gross development value of RM150.86 billion between December 2022 and May 2026. Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu said the achievement was part of the ministry’s efforts to address stalled private housing projects through a task force established in December 2022. Aiman Athirah said the Ministry of Housing and Local Government acted as a mediator between homebuyers and developers to help revive problematic projects.

items.title: Malaysia–Thailand border zone to open up access to Indochina markets, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — The development of the Malaysia–Thailand Border Economic Zone (BEZ) will provide Malaysian goods with wider access to the markets of Laos, Cambodia and Vietnam, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said the BEZ aims to unlock broader economic development beyond Sadao and Bukit Kayu Hitam, which were jointly launched with Thailand’s Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul several days ago. Anwar said the border development initiative would prioritise small and medium-sized enterprises, job creation and skills training to ensure local communities benefit from increased economic activity.

items.title: Ex-IGP Musa Hassan must pay RM250,000 to Hannah Yeoh after Federal Court rejects appeal bid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 14 — The Federal Court here today dismissed former Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Musa Hassan’s application for leave to appeal against the Court of Appeal’s ruling, which ordered him to pay RM250,000 in damages to Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh in a defamation suit. The panel also ordered Musa to pay RM50,000 in costs to Hannah. Hannah was present in court today.

items.title: ‘Meets expectations’: Revised AG-PP separation Bill wins backing from previously dissenting PKR MPs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Several PKR backbenchers who had objected to the original Bill to decouple the public prosecutor’s role from the Attorney General’s Chambers said today they welcomed the revised edition and would back its enactment. Subang MP Wong Chen (PKR) cited the move to allow the House of Representatives to vet candidates for the post of public prosecutor as among the key reasons for their backing of the revised Bill. Wong Chen said he hopes the Bill would be debated alongside the Prime Minister's Term Limit Bill at this sitting.

items.title: Malaysia wants to co-fund scholarships with Indonesia in higher education push
items.summary: JAKARTA, July 14 — Malaysia has proposed exploring co-funded scholarships with Indonesia as part of efforts to strengthen higher education cooperation between the two countries, alongside broader efforts to deepen bilateral academic collaboration. Malaysian Higher Education Ministry Secretary-General Datuk Dr Aminuddin Hassim said the proposal was among several initiatives put forward during a meeting with Indonesian Higher Education, Science and Technology Ministry Secretary-General Prof Badri Munir Sukoco here on Monday. Aminuddin said the proposed arrangement would extend scholarship support to Malaysian students pursuing higher education in Indonesia, complementing Malaysia’s existing scholarship opportunities for Indonesian students pursuing postgraduate studies in Malaysia.
